phase_history.notes: Pre-/dream feasibility spike (plugins/AstroHarmony_Spike/) validated all 4 high-risk technical dependencies inside FL Studio: React 19 boot, Zustand state persistence, @dnd-kit/sortable reorder, JS<->C++ native function bridge via Juce.getNativeFunction. Bundle size baseline 249 KB (Vite + vite-plugin-singlefile produces single-HTML output, bypassing the SimpleGain resource-provider footgun entirely).

1:1 port of the AstroHarmony Electron desktop app (D:/Claude/Sessions/cinematic-composer) into VST3/Standalone instrument plugin. License gate deferred. Full feature inventory including 10-mood engine, 4 sampled instruments (Salamander Piano + Flute + Strings + Trumpet via embedded mp3 BinaryData), host-transport sync, MIDI export, file-based session library at %APPDATA%/AstroHarmony/sessions/.

Architecture: VST3 Instrument, JUCE Synthesiser with mid-flight Sampler swap, juce::dsp::Reverb, host-sync scheduler via AudioPlayHead. Implementation: 7 phased sub-phases A-G with user-verification gate per phase. Total estimate 14-18 days work, 3-4 weeks calendar. Highest remaining risk: Sub-phase D (Sampler audio quality vs Tone.Sampler) — early A/B test planned.

v1-ui-spec.md + v1-style-guide.md + v1-test.html created. Window 1280x800 default (resizable 960x700-1920x1200), cc-dark theme from globals.css :root --cc-* tokens, 4-column CSS grid mirroring WriteWorkspaceV2.tsx exactly: 230px MoodSidebar | flexible main | 5px drag-divider | 450px resizable RightPanel. v1-test.html is a static wireframe ONLY — production HTML comes from Vite/React bundle in /impl Sub-phase B.

Plugin skeleton built and verified in FL Studio. 5.6 MB DLL. APVTS with 5 params (tempo, master_volume, pitch_transpose, loop, bypass). WebView2 loads placeholder card with cc-dark theme. JUCE backend bridge detected. Both validators (setup + member-order) green; setup validator flagged false positives for the single-file Vite architecture which were documented (no separate js/index.js, BinaryData accessed via constant form not getNamedResource()).

Full Vite + React 19 port of all 14 cinematic-composer v2 components. 1.0 MB single-file HTML bundle (vite-plugin-singlefile), 6.6 MB plugin DLL. Side-by-side parity with Electron app verified by user. 3 bugs found and fixed: B.6 (suggest +) + B.7 (voicing-style hang) both caused by stubbed Tone.js await hanging in useChordPreview; fix was to strip Tone.js entirely from audio.ts (Sub-phase D will wire C++ audio through callNative). B.8 (mood→voicing not auto-applying) caused by source's require('@/lib/moodEngine') CommonJS workaround silently failing in Vite ES modules — fix was static import + remove try/catch. No audio at this stage — Sub-phase D adds it.

File-based session library live. Source/io/SessionStore handles list/load/save/delete/rename/duplicate against %APPDATA%/AstroHarmony/sessions/<id>.json. Each file: {id, name, createdAt, updatedAt, data}. JS-side store.ts session methods rewritten — every method routes through callNative now, Zustand sessions[] + activeSessionId act as cache and refresh from C++ after every mutation. Legacy localStorage migration / cc_state_v2 / cc_session_<id> code stripped from store.ts (~250 lines deleted). Third binary_data target (AstroHarmony_Seeds, NAMESPACE Seeds) embeds cinematic-composer/lib/seed-sessions.json (16 KB of curated starters: Sad01 etc.); SessionStore.seedBundledStartersIfNeeded() drops them on disk on first launch, gated by a .seeded marker file so deleted seeds don't come back. Sub-phase E bugs found + fixed: E.5a CMake binary_data strips hyphens entirely when mangling — 'seed-sessions.json' becomes 'seedsessions_json' (not 'seed_sessions_json'); E.5b .seeded marker was written even on failed seeds, locking out retries; E.5c juce::String(const char*, size_t) ctor interprets size as char count and corrupts UTF-8 multi-byte codepoints — switched to String::fromUTF8 so musical symbols ♭/♯/△ survive. User-verified: starter presets show in Projects dropdown, save/load/delete/rename/duplicate all work, presets survive DAW close+reopen, chord names render correctly.

MIDI export wired end-to-end. JS uses existing lib/midi.ts buildMidiFile to produce Format-0 .mid bytes with embedded tempo + time signature + transpose. New exportProgressionAsMidiNative wraps the bytes as a plain number[] (the JUCE bridge marshals via JSON so Uint8Array isn't preserved) and calls native fn exportMidi. C++ side opens juce::FileChooser asynchronously (defaults to userMusicDirectory with the active session name as suggested filename), writes the bytes to the chosen path with replaceWithData. BottomBar EXPORT MIDI button branches on inJuce() — plugin path uses native FileChooser, browser path keeps the legacy Blob download for component dev outside the DLL. User-verified: save dialog opens, .mid drops into FL Studio playlist with correct notes, pitch transpose param affects the exported MIDI as well as audio. Sub-phase F (host transport sync) skipped per user direction — they don't want the plugin tempo locked to DAW transport.

Audio engine live. SampleLibrary lazy-decodes 60 mp3s (Salamander Piano + Flute + VSCO Strings + Trumpet) via juce::MP3AudioFormat from a second binary_data target (NAMESPACE Samples). SamplerEngine: 32-voice juce::Synthesiser with juce::SamplerSound (built via in-memory WAV roundtrip — the only path JUCE 8's SamplerSound exposes), juce::dsp::Reverb (roomSize 0.45 / damping 0.55 / wet 0.14 matching Tone.js source), per-instrument linear gain (strings +16 dB vs piano matching cinematic-composer/lib/instruments.ts), envelope tuned per instrument (attack 10-40 ms, release 80-150 ms). Scheduler advances chord-by-chord on a sample counter, looping optional. JS audio.ts ported to callNative('playChord'|'playProgression'|'stopAll'|'setInstrument'). 4 small native fns (setTempoBpm, setMasterVolumePercent, setTransposeSemis, setLoopEnabled) write APVTS so DAW automation flows both ways. Sub-phase D bugs found + fixed: D.7 BinaryData lookup uses mangled C++ symbol name (piano_C4_mp3) not original filename (piano_C4.mp3); D.7 use-after-free in WAV roundtrip (MemoryOutputStream was stack-allocated, AudioFormatWriter dtor delete'd it); D.8 manual force-stop of same-note voices was redundant with JUCE Synthesiser's built-in behaviour and silenced fresh voices early; D.8 voice stealing on rapid chord retrigger was the real click source — fixed by bumping polyphony 8→32; D.8 auto-release callbacks from old chord killed new chord's freshly-retriggered notes — fixed by generation-counter guard on the Timer callback. User-verified: chord previews audible, instrument switching works, progression playback works, volume balance correct across instruments, rapid same-chord clicks don't click and don't truncate.

JS<->C++ bridge protocol complete. 14 native functions registered in PluginEditor: requestInitialState, pushState (real impls); playChord/playProgression/stopAll/setInstrument (audio stubs, return success — Sub-phase D fills in); listSessions/loadSession/saveCurrentSession/deleteSession/renameSession/duplicateSession (return error — Sub-phase E); exportMidi (error — Sub-phase G); getBuildInfo (real). 30 Hz Timer pushes hostBpmChanged + playStateChanged + currentBeatChanged + stateRestored events via emitEventIfBrowserIsVisible with de-dup (no event spam — only fires on transitions). Processor exposes monotonic customStateBlobSerial atomic so the editor can detect setStateInformation called on a live editor (live preset switch). JS side: jucebridge.ts gained subscribeNative; new lib/stateSync.ts orchestrates hydrate-on-mount via requestInitialState + 200ms debounced pushState on store changes + push-event listeners. Re-entrancy guard (isApplyingFromNative) prevents echo. Verified by user: DAW project save + close + reopen restores full state (progression + mood + scale + voicing + instrument + all UI mode flags).

framework_selection.rationale: Decided pre-/dream based on Phase 0 spike results. Vite + React 19 + Zustand + @dnd-kit + JUCE 8 WebView2 stack proven end-to-end in FL Studio. Single-file bundle via vite-plugin-singlefile sidesteps resource-provider path collision issues (the bug we hit in SimpleGain). React lets us reuse the ~2,200 lines of v2 components from cinematic-composer with minimal modification.

modules_replaced.components/v2/LicenseGate.tsx: Deferred to v1.1 per user decision

phase_0_spike.purpose: Keep as reference for the production /impl phase wiring. Can be removed after AstroHarmony v1 ships.
